VerificationPlan:
[Module][function][BLOCK_NAME] message true Fast deterministic checks per module with trace assertions Integration checks for merged surfaces touched by current wave only Full regression + traceability + fixture corpus validation Never log model API keys, user document content, or raw OOXML payloads exceeding 10KB.
Exact assert on return value, state, or file content hash Required log markers appeared in correct order; forbidden markers absent End-to-end file produced + passes validation pipeline Bounded evaluation against rubric (only where exact assert impossible)
Well-formed DOCX passing all D-H rules sum(columnWidths) != table width for D-H01 WidthType=PERCENTAGE for D-H03 Raw \n in w:t for D-H09 Well-formed PPTX passing all P-H rules Non-embedded system font for P-H02 Minimal docx-js code producing valid DOCX Minimal pptxgenjs code producing valid PPTX Code with require('fs') for sandbox violation test Code with require('net') for sandbox violation test DOCX with GRACE Custom XML Parts already injected DOCX with non-GRACE Custom XML Parts for preservation test
Frontier/medium model writes JS code → sandbox executes → file produced Valid DOCX/PPTX file passes L1 validation, no P0 errors
Sandbox returns within 30s or raises SANDBOX_TIMEOUT Output file is well-formed OOXML (zip + XML parse) No forbidden patterns in executed code
[Create][orchestrate][BLOCK_ORCHESTRATE] [Skills][select][BLOCK_SELECT_SKILL] [Sandbox][execute][BLOCK_EXECUTE_CODE] [Validate][run_checks][BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS] BLOCK_ORCHESTRATE -> BLOCK_SELECT_SKILL -> BLOCK_EXECUTE_CODE -> BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS -> RETURNED
[Sandbox][execute][BLOCK_FS_ACCESS] [Sandbox][execute][BLOCK_NET_ACCESS]
Small model provides JSON → template engine fills master → file produced Valid DOCX/PPTX from template, design tokens applied, no P0 errors
Template output is valid OOXML ZIP All placeholders from template replaced Design token colors/fonts match input
[Create][orchestrate][BLOCK_ORCHESTRATE] [Templates][fill][BLOCK_FILL_TEMPLATE] [Validate][run_checks][BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS] BLOCK_ORCHESTRATE -> BLOCK_FILL_TEMPLATE -> BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS -> RETURNED
Document with violations → validation detects → auto-fix applies safe/visual fixes Safe fixes silent, visual fixes with diff, destructive rejected with hint
Backup file exists before any fix Max 3 fix iterations Destructive fixes never applied Cascade detected if violations not converging
[Validate][run_checks][BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS] [Fix][backup][BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP] [Fix][apply][BLOCK_APPLY_FIX] BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS -> BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP -> BLOCK_APPLY_FIX -> BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS(recheck) -> RETURNED
[Fix][apply] applied destructive fix without DESTRUCTIVE_REJECTED
Existing PPTX/DOCX → style extraction → design-tokens.json JSON contains colors, typography, layout; Layer 1 = 100% accurate
Layer 1 output exactly matches theme/styles.xml content Output JSON is valid against design-tokens schema
[Extract][parse][BLOCK_PARSE_THEME] [Extract][analyze][BLOCK_ANALYZE_LAYOUTS] BLOCK_PARSE_THEME -> BLOCK_ANALYZE_LAYOUTS -> RETURNED
Document → Gotenberg render → overlap/margin/font detection QA report with confidence scores; overlap >2% = violation
L1 completes in <1s L2 confidence score between 0 and 1 Overlap threshold enforced: >0.5% warn, >2% violation
[QA][l1][BLOCK_L1_CHECK] [QA][l2][BLOCK_L2_RENDER] BLOCK_L1_CHECK -> (async) BLOCK_L2_RENDER -> RETURNED
Document → GRACE manifest + instructions injected as Custom XML Parts Metadata readable via OOXML parsers; existing Custom XML preserved
Namespace urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest present in output Non-GRACE Custom XML Parts byte-identical after injection Manifest contains fingerprint field
[GRACE][inject][BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST] [GRACE][preserve][BLOCK_PRESERVE_EXISTING] BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST -> BLOCK_PRESERVE_EXISTING -> RETURNED
tests/unit/test_config.py
pytest tests/unit/test_config.py -x ruff check src/mint/config.py mypy src/mint/config.py
loadConfig reads LLM_BASE_URL + LLM_MODEL + MINT_MODEL_TIER from env → valid MintConfig loadConfig reads .env file when env vars absent → valid MintConfig Missing LLM_BASE_URL raises CONFIG_MISSING with message containing "LLM_BASE_URL" Invalid MINT_MODEL_TIER (e.g. "huge") raises CONFIG_INVALID with valid options listed
Never returns None — must raise or return valid MintConfig Never logs API keys at INFO level
SAFE — no external deps, pure config loading, fully deterministic
tests/unit/test_sandbox.py
pytest tests/unit/test_sandbox.py -x mypy src/mint/sandbox/
execute(hello_world_docx.js) → SandboxResult(success=True, output_path exists, duration_ms > 0) execute(hello_world_pptx.js) → SandboxResult(success=True, output_path exists) execute(malicious_fs.js) → raises SANDBOX_VIOLATION_FS with "fs" in message execute(malicious_net.js) → raises SANDBOX_VIOLATION_NET with "net" in message execute(infinite_loop.js) → SANDBOX_TIMEOUT within 30s + 2s tolerance, BLOCK_EXECUTE_CODE logged execute(syntax_error.js) → SANDBOX_RUNTIME_ERROR with stderr containing error line
[Sandbox][execute][BLOCK_VALIDATE_CODE] [Sandbox][execute][BLOCK_EXECUTE_CODE]
Code with require('fs'), require('net'), require('child_process'), require('http'), require('https') must be rejected in validate_code BEFORE execution process.exit() in user code must be rejected Never write files outside designated output directory
CAUTIOUS — security-critical, requires manual review of sandbox isolation before autonomous execution
tests/unit/test_rules/
pytest tests/unit/test_rules/ -x ruff check src/mint/rules/
D-H01 rule: bad_column_widths.docx → Violation(rule_id="D-H01", severity="hard") D-H03 rule: percentage_width.docx → Violation(rule_id="D-H03", severity="hard", fix_category="destructive") D-H09 rule: raw_newline.docx → Violation(rule_id="D-H09") D-H01..D-H10 rules: minimal_valid.docx → zero violations (0 false positives) D-S01..D-S05 soft rules classify as fix_category safe or visual P-H01..P-H05 rules detect violations in crafted bad PPTX fixtures get_hint("D-H03") → dict with keys: rule, reason, fix_instruction
evaluate must never modify the input document Rule loading must fail fast on invalid YAML schema
SAFE — pure functions, no side effects, fully deterministic
tests/unit/test_validate.py
pytest tests/unit/test_validate.py -x mypy src/mint/validate.py
run_checks(minimal_valid.docx, audit) → ValidationReport(passed=True, violations=[], mode="audit") run_checks(minimal_valid.docx, lenient) → ValidationReport(passed=True) run_checks(bad_column_widths.docx, audit) → ValidationReport(passed=True, hard_count=1) — audit logs but does not reject run_checks(bad_column_widths.docx, lenient) → ValidationReport(passed=False, hard_count=1) run_checks(docx_with_soft_violation, strict) → ValidationReport(passed=False) — strict rejects on warnings run_checks(nonexistent.docx, ...) → raises INVALID_DOCUMENT BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS logged with violation count and mode
[Validate][run_checks][BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS]
Must never modify the input document Must not silently drop violations
SAFE — pure read-only analysis, deterministic
tests/unit/test_fix.py
pytest tests/unit/test_fix.py -x mypy src/mint/fix.py
apply_fixes(docx_with_safe_violation) → FixReport(applied_fixes > 0, backup_path exists, iterations=1) apply_fixes(docx_with_visual_violation) → FixReport with non-empty diff field apply_fixes(docx_with_destructive_violation) → raises DESTRUCTIVE_REJECTED with educational hint apply_fixes(docx_with_cascading_violation, max_iterations=3) → raises CASCADE_DETECTED after 3 iterations BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP logged before BLOCK_APPLY_FIX
[Fix][backup][BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP] [Fix][apply][BLOCK_APPLY_FIX]
Must never fix without creating backup first Must never apply destructive fixes Must stop after 3 iterations even if violations remain
CAUTIOUS — modifies files, but backup + max iterations + destructive rejection provide safety
tests/unit/test_fingerprint.py
pytest tests/unit/test_fingerprint.py -x
compute(minimal_valid.docx) twice → identical hex hash compute(bad_column_widths.docx) != compute(minimal_valid.docx) — different styles.xml compare(hash1, hash1) → DriftStatus.MATCH compare(hash1, hash2) → DriftStatus.DRIFT compare(hash1, None) → DriftStatus.BASELINE_MISSING
SAFE — pure computation, no side effects, fully deterministic
tests/integration/test_mcp_g1.py
pytest tests/integration/test_mcp_g1.py -x
mint_validate(minimal_valid.docx) → JSON with passed=True, violations=[] mint_validate(bad_column_widths.docx) → JSON with passed=False, violations[0].rule_id="D-H01" mint_fix(docx_with_safe_violation) → JSON with applied_fixes, diff, backup_path mint_fingerprint(minimal_valid.docx) → JSON with hash (64-char hex) and drift_status
Verify MCP-G1 works with MCP-G2 mounted (no tool name collisions) SAFE — thin adapter over tested modules
tests/unit/test_skills.py
pytest tests/unit/test_skills.py -x
select_skill(frontier, docx) → file path matching "*frontier*docx*" select_skill(small, pptx) → file path matching "*small*pptx*" select_skill(frontier, xlsx) → raises SKILL_NOT_FOUND render_prompt(skill, design_tokens={...}) → prompt string containing "colors" and "primary"
SAFE — file I/O only, deterministic
tests/unit/test_templates.py
pytest tests/unit/test_templates.py -x
fill(board_deck.pptx, template_content.json) → output.pptx that is valid ZIP with replaced content fill(board_deck.pptx, invalid_json) → raises JSON_SCHEMA_ERROR with field path fill with design_tokens → output font/color matches token values (XPath assertion) list_templates() → list containing at least "board-deck" and "business-memo"
SAFE — file manipulation with validation, deterministic
tests/unit/test_extract.py
pytest tests/unit/test_extract.py -x
extract_style(docx_with_known_theme) → DesignTokens.colors.primary == "#E6007E" (exact match to known fixture) extract_style(docx_with_known_theme) → DesignTokens.typography.heading.font == "Arial" extract_style(complex_pptx) → detected_layouts list with at least 1 entry (statistical, ~80%) extract_style(non_ooxml_file) → raises EXTRACTION_FAILED
[Extract][parse][BLOCK_PARSE_THEME] [Extract][analyze][BLOCK_ANALYZE_LAYOUTS]
SAFE — read-only analysis
tests/integration/test_create.py
pytest tests/integration/test_create.py -x --timeout=60
create(CreateRequest with model_response_override=hello_world_docx.js, tier=frontier) → CreateResult(success=True, execution_mode="code") create(CreateRequest with template_content.json, tier=small, template_name="board-deck") → CreateResult(success=True, execution_mode="template") create with sandbox violation code → CreateResult(success=False, error contains "SANDBOX") BLOCK_ORCHESTRATE → BLOCK_SELECT_SKILL → (BLOCK_EXECUTE_CODE or BLOCK_FILL_TEMPLATE) → BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS all logged
[Create][orchestrate][BLOCK_ORCHESTRATE]
CAUTIOUS — orchestrates multiple modules, depends on external model API
tests/integration/test_mcp_g2.py
pytest tests/integration/test_mcp_g2.py -x --timeout=60
mint_create with code mode → file + validation report mint_extract_style(docx_fixture) → JSON with colors.typography.layout keys mint_list_templates() → non-empty list with name + format fields
Full pipeline test: extract_style → create with tokens → validate → QA SAFE — thin adapter over tested modules
tests/unit/test_qa/test_pipeline.py
pytest tests/unit/test_qa.py -x
run_l1(minimal_valid.docx) → L1Report with passed=True, duration_ms < 1000 run_l1(bad_column_widths.docx) → L1Report with passed=False, violations non-empty run_l2(docx_fixture) with Gotenberg mock → L2Report with confidence score 0.0-1.0 run_l2(docx_fixture) with Gotenberg unavailable → raises GOTENBERG_UNAVAILABLE
[QA][l1][BLOCK_L1_CHECK] [QA][l2][BLOCK_L2_RENDER]
SAFE — L1 is deterministic; L2 depends on Gotenberg availability
tests/unit/test_grace.py
pytest tests/unit/test_grace.py -x
bootstrap(minimal_valid.docx) → output.docx with urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest in Custom XML Parts bootstrap output: manifest contains document_structure + fingerprint fields bootstrap(with_vendor_xml.docx): non-GRACE XML Parts byte-identical before/after (hash comparison) describe(with_grace.docx) → GRACEManifest with instructions list length 5-10
[GRACE][inject][BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST] [GRACE][preserve][BLOCK_PRESERVE_EXISTING]
Must not remove or alter existing Custom XML Parts from other namespaces
SAFE — deterministic XML manipulation
Foundation modules: config loads, sandbox executes valid code and blocks invalid
pytest tests/unit/test_config.py tests/unit/test_sandbox.py -x
Config loads from env vars Sandbox produces valid DOCX from hello_world fixture Sandbox blocks fs and net access
Full validation pipeline: rules → validate → fix → fingerprint
pytest tests/unit/test_rules/ tests/unit/test_validate.py tests/unit/test_fix.py tests/unit/test_fingerprint.py -x
All D-H01..D-H10 rules detect violations in crafted fixtures Validation report correctly classified by severity mode Safe fixes applied, destructive rejected, cascade detected Fingerprint deterministic for same input
MCP G1 tools integration: validate + fix + fingerprint via MCP protocol
pytest tests/integration/test_mcp_g1.py -x
mint_validate returns structured violation report mint_fix returns diff + backup path mint_fingerprint returns hash
Skill prompt selection + template engine basic fill
pytest tests/unit/test_skills.py tests/unit/test_templates.py -x
Full generation pipeline: extract → create → validate → MCP tools
pytest tests/unit/test_extract.py tests/integration/test_create.py tests/integration/test_mcp_g2.py -x --timeout=60
G0 hypothesis validated + G1 deterministic quality gate operational
pytest tests/unit/ -x pytest tests/integration/test_mcp_g1.py -x ruff check src/mint/ mypy src/mint/
Sandbox executes docx-js code without violations All D-H01..D-H10 and P-H01..P-H05 rules have tests with fixtures Auto-fix handles safe/visual/destructive categories correctly StyleFingerprint hash is deterministic for same input 0 false negatives, <5% false positives on test fixture corpus All trace markers logged for VF-003 critical flow
Any model + MINT ≈ Claude document quality (core promise)
pytest tests/ -x --timeout=60 ruff check src/mint/ mypy src/mint/
Skill prompts auto-selected by tier and format Template engine fills JSON → valid DOCX/PPTX Style extraction produces design-tokens.json matching fixture CreateResult.execution_mode correctly set based on tier All trace markers logged for VF-001 and VF-002 critical flows
Visual QA + GRACE metadata + public launch readiness
pytest tests/ -x --timeout=120
QA L2 detects overlap >2% as violation GRACE metadata readable from injected documents Cross-model edit test passes (GLM creates → Qwen edits → MINT validates) All trace markers logged for VF-004, VF-005, VF-006
scenario, expected-evidence, observed-evidence, first-divergent-block, next-action
V-M-FIX scenario-4: cascade detection CASCADE_DETECTED raised after 3 iterations Fix loop ran 4 iterations without raising [Fix][apply][BLOCK_APPLY_FIX] iteration counter not incremented Check iteration counting logic in M-FIX apply_fixes
</VerificationPlan
